frameworks.name: ground-truth validation framework
frameworks.one_line: synthesizing data from known parameters to test whether software recovers them

frameworks.name: population receptive field modeling
frameworks.one_line: pRF fMRI analysis used as the case-study domain for the framework

frameworks.name: computational reproducibility
frameworks.one_line: same-input-same-output guarantees do not ensure scientific correctness

core_question: How can neuroimaging software implementations be validated against ground truth rather than only checked for reproducibility?
key_assumptions: ground-truth synthetic data can be generated from known pRF parameters
computational reproducibility is distinct from computational validity
comparing multiple implementations on shared inputs reveals errors
manipulations: synthesis of fMRI time series from prescribed pRF parameters
running four public pRF tools on standardized inputs
varying realistic acquisition and stimulus conditions
key_findings: framework standardizes inputs/outputs across four pRF implementations
automated reports compare recovered parameters to the ground truth
realistic conditions cause imperfect parameter recovery in all four tools
interpretations: ground-truth synthesis exposes failure modes invisible to classic validation
shared validation frameworks are needed for trustworthy neuroimaging software
